title: OpenAI Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt rendered in BuilderView; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**OpenAI** is the foundational LLM + multimodal AI platform exposing a REST API at `https://api.openai.com/v1`. This connector ‚Äî `OpenAIConnector` (class constant `CONNECTOR_TYPE = "openai"`) ‚Äî wraps every operational surface a Shielva tenant typically needs from OpenAI under a single API key:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Models | `/v1/models` | List + describe models the API key has access to |
| Chat Completions | `/v1/chat/completions` | Run a chat completion (GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, o1, gpt-3.5-turbo) |
| Embeddings | `/v1/embeddings` | Vectorise text (text-embedding-3-small/large) |
| Files | `/v1/files` | Upload / list / delete files used by Assistants, fine-tune, batch |
| Images | `/v1/images/generations` | Generate images (DALL¬∑E 2/3, gpt-image-1) |
| Speech (TTS) | `/v1/audio/speech` | Synthesise speech audio (tts-1, tts-1-hd) |
| Audio (Whisper) | `/v1/audio/transcriptions` | Speech-to-text (whisper-1) |
| Moderations | `/v1/moderations` | Policy-violation classifier (text-moderation-latest) |

The connector exposes 11 public async API methods + lifecycle (`install`, `health_check`, `sync`, `authorize`) + 4 webhook/event handler stubs (`handle_webhook`, `process_callback`, `handle_event`, `batch_processor`). It is fully multi-tenant: any `NormalizedDocument` produced uses `id = f"{tenant_id}_{source_id}"`.

Who uses it: any tenant on the Shielva platform that needs GPT-4o chat, embedding-based RAG, Whisper transcription, DALL¬∑E image generation, or TTS audio synthesis ‚Äî agent builders, knowledge-base ingestion pipelines, voice frontends, content-moderation flows.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Provision an OpenAI API key

1. Sign in to https://platform.openai.com/api-keys.
2. Click **Create new secret key**, name it (e.g. `shielva-prod`), and choose a project.
3. Copy the key (`sk-‚Ä¶`) ‚Äî OpenAI shows it only once at creation.
4. (Optional) From **Settings ‚Üí Organization** grab your `org-‚Ä¶` id if you need org-scoped requests.

### 2. Install the connector in ACP

In **ACP ‚Üí Manage Connectors**, choose **OpenAI**. Fill in:

| Install field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret, required) | The `sk-‚Ä¶` key from step 1 |
| `base_url` (text, optional) | Default `https://api.openai.com/v1`. Override for Azure or proxies. |
| `organization_id` (text, optional) | `org-‚Ä¶` if you have multiple orgs |
| `timeout_s` (number, optional) | HTTP timeout in seconds ‚Äî default 60 |
| `webhook_secret` (secret, optional) | HMAC-SHA256 secret for signed callback verification |

Click **Install**. The connector calls `install()` which validates `api_key` is present (it does **not** call the OpenAI API at this point) and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, AUTHENTICATED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` next. That probes `GET https://api.openai.com/v1/models?limit=1` with `Authorization: Bearer <key>`. A 2xx ‚Üí `HEALTHY+CONNECTED`. 401 ‚Üí `TOKEN_EXPIRED+OFFLINE`. 403 ‚Üí `INVALID_CREDENTIALS+UNHEALTHY`. 429 ‚Üí `DEGRADED+CONNECTED` with warning logged.

### 4. First call

A chat completion in three lines:

```python
await connector.create_chat_completion(
    model="gpt-4o-mini",
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": "Hello"}],
)
```

The response is a flat dict ‚Äî `content` (assistant text), `model`, `finish_reason`, `usage`, plus `raw` for the full OpenAI envelope.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
OpenAI uses a single **API key** sent as a Bearer token ‚Äî `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`. There is no OAuth flow, no token exchange, and (by default) no automatic expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` (required) ‚Äî the `sk-‚Ä¶` key. Encoded into the `Authorization` header:
  ```
  Authorization: Bearer sk-‚Ä¶
  ```
- `organization_id` (optional) ‚Äî when set, sent as a separate header:
  ```
  OpenAI-Organization: org-‚Ä¶
  ```
- `base_url` (optional) ‚Äî defaults to `https://api.openai.com/v1`. Override for Azure-compatible proxies or OpenAI-compatible self-hosted endpoints.

Headers are built once in `client/http_client.py::OpenAIHTTPClient._headers()` and applied to every outbound request.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key` is non-empty in `self.config`. Does **not** call any OpenAI endpoint. Returns `HEALTHY + AUTHENTICATED` on validation success. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` wrapper whose `access_token` is the configured `api_key` (compatibility shim ‚Äî no token exchange happens). |
| `health_check()` | Lightweight probe against `GET /v1/models?limit=1`. Maps 401/403/429 via `_STATUS_MAP`. |
| token storage | None. The API key is held in `self.config`, persisted by `save_config()` to the gateway's encrypted store. |
| key rotation | Re-run install with a new `sk-‚Ä¶` value. There is no refresh flow. |

### Scopes / permissions

OpenAI keys are scoped at creation time in the dashboard: **All / Restricted (read-only)**. If `health_check()` returns `403 INVALID_CREDENTIALS`, the key likely lacks `model.read` permission ‚Äî recreate the key with **All** scope.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Defined in `metadata/connector.json::install_fields` and read via `self.config.get(...)` at runtime ‚Äî **never** hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `connector.py::__init__` ‚Üí `self.api_key` | Bearer token in `Authorization` |
| `base_url` | string | no | `connector.py::__init__` ‚Üí `self.base_url` | Override for Azure / proxy |
| `organization_id` | string | no | `connector.py::__init__` ‚Üí `self.organization_id` | `OpenAI-Organization` header |
| `timeout_s` | number | no | `connector.py::__init__` ‚Üí `self.timeout_s` | Per-request httpx timeout |
| `webhook_secret` | secret | no | `connector.py::process_callback` ‚Üí `self.config.get("webhook_secret")` | HMAC-SHA256 secret for callback verification |

### Class-level constants

Provider-wide values that never vary per tenant:

```python
# client/http_client.py
OPENAI_BASE_URL = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
```

```python
# connector.py
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE", "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED", "CONNECTED"),
}
_WEBHOOK_EVENT_TYPES = [
    "response.completed", "response.failed",
    "thread.message.completed", "thread.run.completed", "thread.run.failed",
    "batch.completed", "batch.failed",
    "fine_tuning.job.succeeded", "fine_tuning.job.failed",
]
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content:
11 public async API methods + lifecycle + handlers. Each method is a standalone `async def` in `connector.py` (OCP ‚Äî none folded into `sync()`). All HTTP delegated to `OpenAIHTTPClient.request(...)` (SOC).

Grouped by surface; full param + return docs in `metadata/connector.json::apis`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS` are all present. Persist via `save_config()`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code='', state='') -> TokenInfo`
Compatibility shim: returns `TokenInfo(access_token=self.api_key, token_type='api_key')`. No real OAuth exchange.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probe `GET /v1/models?limit=1`. Classify failures via `_STATUS_MAP`. Returns `HEALTHY+CONNECTED` on 2xx.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
List `/v1/files` and ingest each as a `NormalizedDocument` (tenant-scoped id). OpenAI is an LLM provider ‚Äî when no files are uploaded, returns `SyncResult(SUCCESS, 0/0/0)` with message `"sync not applicable for LLM provider (no files)"`.

children.title: Chat Completions
children.content:
### `async create_chat_completion(model, messages, temperature=1.0, max_tokens=1024, **kwargs) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/chat/completions`. Body: `{model, messages, temperature, max_tokens, ...kwargs}`.

Returns the normalised response from `helpers.utils.normalize_chat_response`:

```python
{
    "id": "chatcmpl-‚Ä¶",
    "model": "gpt-4o-mini",
    "content": "Hello! How can I help?",
    "role": "assistant",
    "finish_reason": "stop",
    "usage": {"prompt_tokens": 5, "completion_tokens": 8, "total_tokens": 13},
    "raw": { ... full OpenAI envelope ... }
}
```

Extra OpenAI parameters (`top_p`, `stream`, `tools`, `tool_choice`, `response_format`, `seed`, `frequency_penalty`, etc.) are forwarded via `**kwargs`.

children.title: Embeddings
children.content:
### `async create_embedding(model, input, dimensions=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/embeddings`. Body: `{model, input, dimensions?}`.

`input` can be a string or a list of strings (batched embeddings). When using `text-embedding-3-small` or `text-embedding-3-large`, pass `dimensions=‚Ä¶` to request a shortened vector (useful for pgvector storage). Returns the raw OpenAI response ‚Äî the vector is at `response['data'][0]['embedding']`.

Use `helpers/utils.py::extract_embedding_vector(raw)` to pull the first vector as a flat `List[float]`.

children.title: Models
children.content:
### `async list_models() -> Dict`
`GET /v1/models`. Returns `{object: "list", data: [{id, object, created, owned_by}, ...]}`.

### `async get_model(model_id) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/models/{model_id}`. Raises `OpenAIError` if `model_id` is empty. Returns 404 from OpenAI for unknown models, which surfaces as `OpenAINotFoundError`.

children.title: Files
children.content:
### `async list_files(purpose=None) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/files`. When `purpose` is set, filters server-side. Returns `{data: [{id, filename, bytes, purpose, status, created_at}, ...]}`.

### `async upload_file(purpose, file_name, content: bytes) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/files` ‚Äî multipart with `file=(file_name, content)` + `purpose` form field. Valid purposes: `assistants`, `batch`, `fine-tune`, `vision`, `user_data`. Returns the new file record (includes `id`).

### `async delete_file(file_id) -> Dict`
`DELETE /v1/files/{file_id}`. Returns `{id, deleted: True}` (placeholder if the server returns an empty body).

children.title: Images
children.content:
### `async create_image(prompt, model='dall-e-3', size='1024x1024', n=1, **kwargs) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/images/generations`. Body: `{model, prompt, size, n, ...kwargs}`.

For `dall-e-3` use `n=1` (the API rejects higher) and sizes `1024x1024`, `1024x1792`, `1792x1024`. For `gpt-image-1` pass `quality='high'` via `**kwargs`. Returns `{created, data: [{url} | {b64_json}]}`.

children.title: Audio (TTS + Whisper)
children.content:
### `async create_speech(model, voice, input, response_format='mp3') -> bytes`
`POST /v1/audio/speech` ‚Äî synthesise speech. Voices: `alloy`, `echo`, `fable`, `onyx`, `nova`, `shimmer`. Formats: `mp3`, `opus`, `aac`, `flac`, `wav`, `pcm`. Returns the **raw audio bytes** (not JSON).

### `async create_transcription(file_name, content: bytes, model='whisper-1', response_format='json', language=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/audio/transcriptions` ‚Äî multipart audio file ‚Üí text. `language` is an optional ISO-639-1 hint. Returns `{text: "..."}` (when `response_format=json`) or `{text}` with extra metadata for `verbose_json`.

children.title: Moderation
children.content:
### `async create_moderation(input, model='text-moderation-latest') -> Dict`
`POST /v1/moderations`. Returns `{id, model, results: [{flagged, categories, category_scores}]}`. Use the per-category scores (0‚Äì1) rather than the binary `flagged` flag for nuanced policy decisions.

children.title: Webhook + Event Handlers
children.content:
BaseConnector lifecycle overrides ‚Äî signatures match exactly.

### `async handle_webhook(payload, headers=None) -> Dict`
If `webhook_secret` is configured ‚Üí calls `process_callback` for signature verification (returns `{status: 'error'}` on mismatch). Then matches `payload['type']` against the recognised set:
- `response.completed`, `response.failed` (Realtime / Responses API)
- `thread.message.completed`, `thread.run.completed`, `thread.run.failed` (Assistants API)
- `batch.completed`, `batch.failed` (Batch API)
- `fine_tuning.job.succeeded`, `fine_tuning.job.failed`

Unknown event ‚Üí `{status: 'ignored', event_type: ...}`.

### `async process_callback(payload, headers=None) -> Dict`
Computes `HMAC-SHA256(payload, webhook_secret)` and compares with `OpenAI-Signature` header via `hmac.compare_digest` (timing-safe). Returns `{verified: True, data: payload}` on success, `{verified: False, error: 'signature_mismatch'}` otherwise. When no secret is configured ‚Üí returns `{verified: True, unverified: True}` so the caller decides policy.

### `async handle_event(event) -> Dict`
Idempotency-keyed event ack. Returns `{event_id, processed: True}`.

### `async batch_processor(items, **kwargs) -> Dict`
Iterates through events, catches per-item exceptions, returns `{processed: N, failed: N, errors: [...]}`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
OpenAIError
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ OpenAIAuthError         (401 / 403)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ OpenAIBadRequestError   (400)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ OpenAINotFoundError     (404)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ OpenAIConflictError     (409)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ OpenAIRateLimitError    (429 ‚Äî carries retry_after_s)
‚îú‚îÄ‚îÄ OpenAIServerError       (5xx)
‚îî‚îÄ‚îÄ OpenAINetworkError      (DNS / TLS / timeout)
```

Each exception carries `.message`, `.status_code`, and `.response_body` (parsed JSON error envelope from OpenAI).

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::request`)

| Status / cause | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately ‚Äî not retryable |
| 429 | Sleep `Retry-After` seconds (server-provided), retry up to `max_retries=3` |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff `min(2 ** attempt, 8)`, retry up to `max_retries=3` |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` | Exponential backoff, retry up to `max_retries=3` |
| Other `httpx.RequestError` (DNS / connection) | Raise `OpenAINetworkError` immediately |

### How errors surface to the gateway

In `health_check()` and `sync()`, exceptions are caught at the lifecycle boundary and mapped via `_classify_failure`:

```
status_code ‚Üí (ConnectorHealth, AuthStatus):
   401 ‚Üí (OFFLINE,   TOKEN_EXPIRED)
   403 ‚Üí (UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
   429 ‚Üí (DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)  ‚Äî logged as warning
   network ‚Üí (OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
   other   ‚Üí (UNHEALTHY, FAILED)
```

The gateway sees a `ConnectorStatus` with a meaningful `health` + `auth_status` rather than an unhandled stacktrace.

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` is blank | Re-enter the `sk-‚Ä¶` key in ACP. Keys are shown only once at creation in the OpenAI dashboard ‚Äî if lost, rotate the key. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 from OpenAI ‚Äî invalid / revoked key | Verify the key still exists in https://platform.openai.com/api-keys. If it was deleted, generate a new one. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from OpenAI ‚Äî key lacks `model.read` permission or org mismatch | Recreate the key with **All** permissions, or verify the configured `organization_id` matches the key's owning org. |
| Repeated `openai.http.rate_limited` warnings | Hitting 429 (rate / quota limit) | The client honours `Retry-After`, but if 429s persist you're hitting your account RPM / TPM cap ‚Äî request a quota increase or throttle upstream. |
| `create_chat_completion` returns `finish_reason: "length"` | Output truncated at `max_tokens` | Increase `max_tokens` or trim the prompt. |
| `create_chat_completion` returns `finish_reason: "content_filter"` | Response blocked by OpenAI's safety system | Reframe the prompt or call `create_moderation` first to detect upstream. |
| `upload_file` raises `OpenAIError: purpose must be one of ‚Ä¶` | Invalid purpose value | Use one of `assistants`, `batch`, `fine-tune`, `vision`, `user_data`. |
| `create_speech` returns empty bytes | Wrong `response_format` or 4xx silently swallowed in a misconfigured retry shim | Inspect `OpenAIError.response_body` ‚Äî OpenAI describes invalid formats explicitly. |
| Webhooks land but `process_callback` returns `verified: False` | `webhook_secret` mismatch with the value configured in the OpenAI dashboard | Re-enter the exact same secret in both places (no leading/trailing whitespace). |
| `from connector import OpenAIConnector` fails in test | conftest.py missing `sys.path.insert(0, parent)` | Already fixed ‚Äî `tests/conftest.py:25` inserts the package root onto sys.path. |
| Azure-OpenAI requests get 404 / wrong shape | `base_url` not overridden | Set `base_url` to your Azure deployment's `/openai/deployments/<deployment>/` endpoint and supply the matching `api-version` query param via the request kwargs. |
